evt_014.label: Drop
evt_014.basis: Company G stated that it would not continue in the process, with no filing-grounded valuation comparison or target exclusion.
evt_014.source_text: Also on May 3, 2013, Company G indicated it would not continue in the process.

evt_019.label: DropAtInf
evt_019.basis: Company H said it could not improve the value range from its earlier informal proposal, so the bidder dropped at its prior informal level.
evt_019.source_text: Company H was not able to increase its indicated value range.

evt_023.label: Drop
evt_023.basis: WDC reevaluated its interest and said it was not prepared to move forward, which is a bidder-initiated withdrawal rather than a comparative price drop or target exclusion.
evt_023.source_text: On May 31, 2013, a representative of WDC communicated to BofA Merrill Lynch that WDC was reevaluating its interest in acquiring sTec, and that WDC was not prepared to move forward with a transaction.

evt_024.label: Drop
evt_024.basis: Company D said it could not actively continue diligence and was disengaging from the process, with no filing-grounded price comparison.
evt_024.source_text: Company D reconfirmed that it would not be in a position to actively conduct due diligence for more than two weeks and was disengaging from the process.

cycle_1.basis: No formal proposals in this cycle.

initiation_judgment.basis: The process combined board-driven exploration of strategic alternatives with contemporaneous inbound bidder interest and later activist pressure, rather than reflecting a single clear trigger.
initiation_judgment.source_text: Consequently, in mid-November, 2012, our board of directors authorized management to contact potential financial advisors to explore their interest in representing the company in its exploration of strategic alternatives.

advisor_bofa_merrill_lynch.source_text: the board approved retaining BofA Merrill Lynch to act as sTec's financial advisor

advisor_gibson_dunn.source_text: Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher LLP, which had been engaged in October 2012 as the company's outside corporate counsel

advisor_shearman.source_text: Shearman & Sterling LLP, counsel to WDC

count_reconciliation.assertion: BofA Merrill Lynch had discussions with 18 prospective acquirers.
count_reconciliation.note: The extract names nine bidder actors individually (WDC and Companies A-H), leaving nine contacted counterparties unnamed in the filing chronology.

count_reconciliation.assertion: sTec executed six non-disclosure agreements related to the exploration of a potential sale of the company.
count_reconciliation.note: Counts match exactly across the six NDA signers extracted in the chronology.

count_reconciliation.assertion: Three prospective acquirers submitted written whole-company indications of interest.
count_reconciliation.note: Counts match exactly across WDC, Company D, and Company H.
